SELECT
  (SELECT AVG(f.Consumption)
   FROM filtered f
   JOIN customers c ON f.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
   WHERE c.Segment = 'SME') -
  (SELECT AVG(f.Consumption)
   FROM filtered f
   JOIN customers c ON f.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
   WHERE c.Segment = 'LAM') AS diff_SME_LAM,
  (SELECT AVG(f.Consumption)
   FROM filtered f
   JOIN customers c ON f.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
   WHERE c.Segment = 'LAM') -
  (SELECT AVG(f.Consumption)
   FROM filtered f
   JOIN customers c ON f.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
   WHERE c.Segment = 'KAM') AS diff_LAM_KAM,
  (SELECT AVG(f.Consumption)
   FROM filtered f
   JOIN customers c ON f.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
   WHERE c.Segment = 'KAM') -
  (SELECT AVG(f.Consumption)
   FROM filtered f
   JOIN customers c ON f.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
   WHERE c.Segment = 'SME') AS diff_KAM_SME
FROM (
  SELECT ym.CustomerID, ym.Consumption
  FROM yearmonth ym
  JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
  WHERE c.Currency = 'CZK'
    AND ym.Date LIKE '2013-%'
    AND ym.Consumption = (
      SELECT MIN(ym2.Consumption)
      FROM yearmonth ym2
      JOIN customers c2 ON ym2.CustomerID = c2.CustomerID
      WHERE c2.Currency = 'CZK'
        AND ym2.Date LIKE '2013-%'
    )
) AS filtered;	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing
7:
SELECT 'max' AS type, Segment, growth
FROM (
    SELECT Segment,
           (t2013.total - t2012.total) / t2012.total * 100.0 AS growth
    FROM (
        SELECT c.Segment, substr(ym.Date, 1, 4) AS year, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total
        FROM yearmonth ym
        JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
        WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
        GROUP BY c.Segment, year
    ) t2013
    JOIN (
        SELECT c.Segment, substr(ym.Date, 1, 4) AS year, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total
        FROM yearmonth ym
        JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
        WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
        GROUP BY c.Segment, year
    ) t2012
      ON t2013.Segment = t2012.Segment
    WHERE t2013.year = '2013' AND t2012.year = '2012'
) AS g
ORDER BY growth DESC
LIMIT 1
UNION ALL
SELECT 'min' AS type, Segment, growth
FROM (
    SELECT Segment,
           (t2013.total - t2012.total) / t2012.total * 100.0 AS growth
    FROM (
        SELECT c.Segment, substr(ym.Date, 1, 4) AS year, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total
        FROM yearmonth ym
        JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
        WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
        GROUP BY c.Segment, year
    ) t2013
    JOIN (
        SELECT c.Segment, substr(ym.Date, 1, 4) AS year, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total
        FROM yearmonth ym
        JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
        WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
        GROUP BY c.Segment, year
    ) t2012
      ON t2013.Segment = t2012.Segment
    WHERE t2013.year = '2013' AND t2012.year = '2012'
) AS g
ORDER BY growth ASC
LIMIT 1;	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

SELECT f.account_id, (ma.max_avg - mf.min_avg) AS diff
FROM (
    SELECT ca.client_id, ca.account_id, ca.avg_income
    FROM (
        SELECT c.client_id, d.account_id, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_income
        FROM client c
        JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
        JOIN trans t ON d.account_id = t.account_id
        GROUP BY c.client_id, d.account_id
    ) ca
    JOIN client c ON ca.client_id = c.client_id
    WHERE c.gender = 'F'
    ORDER BY c.birth_date ASC
    LIMIT 1
) f
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT MIN(avg_income) AS min_avg
    FROM (
        SELECT c.client_id, d.account_id, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_income
        FROM client c
        JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
        JOIN trans t ON d.account_id = t.account_id
        GROUP BY c.client_id, d.account_id
    ) ca
    JOIN client c ON ca.client_id = c.client_id
    WHERE c.gender = 'F'
) mf
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT MAX(avg_income) AS max_avg
    FROM (
        SELECT c.client_id, d.account_id, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_income
        FROM client c
        JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
        JOIN trans t ON d.account_id = t.account_id
        GROUP BY c.client_id, d.account_id
    ) ca
) ma;	----- bird -----	financial
